channel.title: networking-tools on james
channel.description: Recent content in networking-tools on james

item.title: nmap
item.description: general filtered = unsure if open or closed up arrow to check status if in progress nmap <args> <ip_address> # default: top 1000 ports -F # fast (100 ports) -p # ports -p- # all ports --top-ports X # X top ports --open # only open -s<> # scan type -sS # stealth -sX # XMAS - rst if closed, nothing if open -sF # FIN - rst if closed, nothing if open -sN # NULL - rst if closed, nothing if open -sA # ACK - test if there is firewall (no firewall = rst, firewall = no response) -sU # UDP --script ipidseq <ip/24> # to find zombie - Incremental -sI <zombie_id> <target_id> # idle scan -Pn # open ports only -A # aggressive; lots of stuff inc OS; very noisy -sV # software/service version -O # operating system -vvv # (very) (very) verbose -n # only ips not names --reason # why nmap says something -L iplist.

item.title: wireshark
item.description: capture filter dst 192.168.1.8 and port 443 src 192.168.1.1 and not port 80 host wireshark.org filter # ips ip.addr == 192.168.1.1 ip.src == 192.168.1.1 and ip.dst == 192.168.1.8 # ports tcp.port == 443 tcp.srcport == 443 # protocols http http.requests.get == 'post' tcp tcp.flags.syn==1 # handshakes arp dns ssh # not, these are different !ip.addr == 192.168.1.1 ip.addr != 192.168.1.1 # frame frame contains “(attachment|tar|exe|zip|pdf)” # keywords tcp contains facebook frame contains facebook # syn floods tcp.
